_doc: 200 distinct TLA+ specification topics for parallel diamond generation. Each batch is assigned to one Opus 4.6 subagent. Topics are chosen to cover the breadth of tlaplus/examples — protocols, concurrency, data structures, classic CS — with enough variety that agents do not collide. Module names are SUGGESTED; agents can rename for clarity but must keep them unique within their batch.

topics.desc: A binary semaphore protecting a critical section: P() blocks while held, V() releases. Track holder count (0 or 1) and a bounded number of waiters. Safety: at most one holder.
Counting semaphore with capacity K. Acquire decrements (blocks at 0), release increments (capped at K). Safety: count stays in 0..K and acquired processes is bounded.
Reader-writer lock. Multiple readers OR one writer. Track reader_count and writer_held. Safety: writer_held implies reader_count = 0; reader_count > 0 implies not writer_held.
N-thread barrier: each thread arrives, increments count, blocks until count = N, then all proceed and count resets. Safety: any thread is past the barrier only if all N have arrived.
Java-style countdown latch initialized to N. countDown decrements; await blocks until counter = 0. Safety: once latched-open it stays open (monotone).
Plain mutex with TryLock action that may fail. Track owner (NONE or process id). Safety: at most one owner at any time; release only by owner.
Reentrant mutex: holder may re-acquire, increasing depth. Release decrements depth; lock free when depth = 0. Safety: only the holder may release.
Hoare-style monitor with one condition variable. wait() releases lock and blocks; signal() wakes one waiter. Track lock holder and wait queue size. Safety: at most one thread inside monitor.
Linux-style futex: atomic compare-and-wait on a memory word. Track word value and number of sleepers. wake(n) wakes up to n sleepers. Safety: sleepers <= total threads.
Test-and-set spin lock. Threads loop CASing 0->1; release writes 0. Track lock state and which threads are spinning vs holding. Safety: at most one holder.
Ticket lock: each thread takes a ticket (next_ticket++) and waits until now_serving = its ticket. Track next_ticket, now_serving, and ticket-per-thread. Safety: at most one holder; FIFO order.
MCS queue lock: each thread enqueues a node and spins on its predecessor. Model as a bounded FIFO queue of waiting thread ids. Safety: head of queue is the unique holder.
sync.Once-style: a one-shot initializer. First caller runs the action; all others wait then return. Track state in {pending, running, done}. Safety: action runs at most once.
Go-style WaitGroup. Add(n) increments counter; Done() decrements; Wait blocks until 0. Safety: counter never negative; waiters released only when counter = 0.
Bounded synchronous channel of capacity K. send blocks when full, recv blocks when empty. Track buffer length. Safety: 0 <= len <= K; never recv from empty.
Bounded model of an unbounded channel (cap by Max for finite checking). send always succeeds; recv blocks when empty. Safety: pending = sent - received.
Single-assignment future: producer fulfills with a value, consumers read. Track state in {empty, fulfilled}. Safety: at most one fulfill; reads after fulfill see the value.
Three-stage pipeline with bounded queues between stages. Each stage moves items forward. Safety: each queue stays within its capacity; items conserved (in = sum_queues + out).
Two-worker work-stealing model. Each worker has a local deque; idle worker steals from the other's tail. Safety: total tasks conserved; no task processed twice.
Producer-consumer with bounded buffer of capacity K. Producer blocks when full; consumer blocks when empty. Safety: 0 <= buffer <= K; produced - consumed = buffer.
Peterson's algorithm for two processes. flag[0], flag[1] booleans and turn variable. Safety: at most one process in critical section.
Dekker's algorithm for two processes using flag and turn, with the inner spin/yield logic. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Lamport's bakery algorithm for N processes with choosing[] and number[] arrays. Safety: at most one process in critical section.
Dijkstra's original mutex for N processes with k, b[], c[] variables. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Szymanski's mutex algorithm: each process tracks a level 0..4. Process enters CS only when no others at conflicting levels. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Lamport's fast mutex (1987): X, Y variables and per-process b[]. Optimized for the uncontended case. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Anderson's array-based queueing mutex: each process spins on its own slot in a circular array. Safety: mutual exclusion; FIFO.
Simple two-process handshake using two booleans (req_a, req_b). Process enters CS only after observing the other has not requested. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Mutex built on a single test-and-set bit. Threads spin until the bit was 0. Safety: at most one holder.
Mutex built from fetch-and-add producing a ticket; holder is the one whose ticket equals serving counter. Safety: at most one holder; FIFO.
Reed-Kanodia event count: advance() increments a counter; await(v) waits until counter >= v. Safety: counter monotone non-decreasing.

Variant of bakery generalized to readers and writers: writer needs the smallest ticket and zero readers; readers need no writer with a smaller ticket. Safety: no writer concurrent with another writer or any reader.
Priority-ceiling protocol for two priorities and one shared mutex. A holder inherits the ceiling priority. Safety: no priority inversion possible; mutual exclusion.
Tournament tree mutex over 2^k processes, built from 2-process Peterson nodes. Track which process is at each tree node. Safety: at most one process at the root.
Burns' mutex for N processes using a single flag array and a fixed priority order. Safety: mutual exclusion.
Aravind's local-spin mutex variant. Each process has a private wait flag set by the previous holder. Safety: mutual exclusion; bounded waiting.
Mutex with a TryLockTimeout action that gives up after a bounded number of attempts. Safety: at most one holder; timeout return implies lock was held by another at the time.
Mutex with explicit fairness: a turn pointer rotates among waiters. Safety: mutual exclusion. Liveness: bounded bypass.
Recursive mutex with depth counter. Same thread may re-enter; release at depth 0 frees lock. Safety: only the holder may decrement; depth >= 0.
Adaptive mutex: short spin, then sleep. Track per-thread mode in {spinning, sleeping, holding}. Safety: at most one holder.

Bully leader-election algorithm over N processes with totally ordered ids. Higher ids preempt lower ones. Track per-process state in {follower, candidate, leader} and elected leader. Safety: at most one leader at a time.
Chang-Roberts ring leader election. Each process forwards the largest id it has seen; the process whose own id returns is the leader. Safety: at most one leader; the leader has the maximum id.
Hirschberg-Sinclair O(N log N) ring election. Processes send probes outward, doubling distance each phase. Safety: unique leader = max id.
Simple ring election: token carries the maximum id seen so far around the ring once. Safety: leader = max id.
Two-phase commit with one coordinator and N participants. Coordinator collects votes, then decides commit if all yes. Safety: no two participants decide differently.
Three-phase commit adding a pre-commit stage to avoid coordinator-blocking. Safety: agreement on the final decision.
Synchronous flooding consensus over N nodes for f crash failures. Each node broadcasts its set; after f+1 rounds all alive nodes agree. Safety: validity and agreement.
Single-decree Paxos: proposers prepare with ballot numbers, acceptors promise / accept, learners learn. Safety: at most one value chosen.
Fast Paxos single-decree variant where clients send directly to acceptors in the fast round. Safety: agreement on chosen value.
Quorum-based write: a write succeeds when w out of N replicas ack. Track per-replica accepted value. Safety: any two majority quorums intersect on a common chosen value.
Epidemic gossip: each round each node picks a peer and copies its state. Track per-node infected bit. Safety: state monotone (only spreads). Liveness: eventually all infected.
Simple majority voting among N voters with two candidates. Track per-voter ballot and tally. Safety: declared winner has > N/2 votes.
Raft leader-election subset: terms, RequestVote, granted-votes set. A candidate becomes leader at majority. Safety: at most one leader per term.
View-change protocol: each node proposes a new view number; a new view installs when a quorum of view-change messages arrive. Safety: at most one primary per view.
Byzantine quorum write: w >= 2f+1 acks out of 3f+1 replicas. Track honest acks and Byzantine equivocations. Safety: honest learners agree.
Generic atomic commit gate: all participants must agree to commit, or all abort. Track per-participant vote and decision. Safety: uniform agreement.
2PC variant with coordinator crash and a backup that takes over on suspicion. Safety: no participant ever sees conflicting decisions.
Epoch-numbered leader election: a node may be leader only in its own epoch and only if its epoch is the highest seen. Safety: at most one leader per epoch.
Dolev-Strong synchronous Byzantine broadcast for f faults: each round forwards values that have collected f+1 distinct signatures. Safety: validity and agreement among honest nodes.
Primary-backup with a witness replica that breaks ties. Track per-replica state and committed log length. Safety: committed prefix is the same on majority.
Alternating bit protocol over a lossy channel. Sender alternates a bit; receiver acks with the bit it last accepted. Safety: receiver delivers each value exactly once in order.
Sliding-window protocol with window size W over a reliable channel. Track sender base, next, receiver expected. Safety: in-order delivery; gap <= W.
Go-back-N retransmission: on timeout sender retransmits all unacked frames in window. Safety: receiver delivery is a prefix of sender input.
Selective-repeat ARQ: sender retransmits only the lost frame. Receiver buffers out-of-order frames. Safety: in-order delivery.
Stop-and-wait protocol over a possibly-duplicating channel. Sender sends one frame, waits for ack, then advances. Safety: each frame delivered exactly once.
TCP three-way handshake: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK. Track per-endpoint state in {closed, syn_sent, syn_recv, established}. Safety: only matched halves enter established.
TCP four-way close (FIN/ACK/FIN/ACK). State per endpoint in {established, fin_wait, close_wait, last_ack, time_wait, closed}. Safety: both peers reach closed only along legal transitions.
ARP request/reply with a bounded cache. On miss, broadcast request, await reply, cache it. Safety: cache entries are consistent with replied bindings.
DHCP DORA: discover, offer, request, ack. A bounded address pool. Safety: no two clients hold the same address simultaneously.
Token ring: a single token circulates among N stations; only the holder may transmit. Safety: at most one transmitting station; token always exists.
CSMA/CD: stations sense the channel, transmit if idle, back off on collision. Track channel state and per-station mode. Safety: collisions are detected and retransmitted.
Heartbeat-based failure detector: each node periodically heartbeats; a peer that misses K consecutive heartbeats is marked suspected. Safety: a node marked alive sent a heartbeat within the window.
Vector clocks for N processes. Each event ticks own entry; a send copies the vector; on receive, take elementwise max and tick. Safety: clocks monotone; happens-before respected.
Lamport scalar clocks. Each event increments; receives take max(local, sent)+1. Safety: clocks monotone; if a happens-before b then clock(a) < clock(b).
Causal broadcast over reliable links: messages tagged with vector timestamp; deliver only when causal predecessors are delivered. Safety: causal order preserved.
ABD-style read/write register replicated on N nodes with majority quorums. Reads do read-impose. Safety: linearizability of register operations.
Total-order multicast via a sequencer node. Sequencer assigns monotone numbers; receivers deliver in order. Safety: all receivers deliver in identical order.
Chandy-Lamport global snapshot for two processes and one channel. Track marker propagation and recorded local + channel states. Safety: snapshot is a consistent cut.
Bounded exponential backoff: each failure doubles the delay up to a max. Track attempt count and current delay. Safety: attempts <= MAX; delay in {1, 2, 4, ..., MaxDelay}.
Circuit breaker with closed/open/half-open states. Failures trip from closed to open; a probe in half-open closes or re-opens. Safety: half-open admits at most one probe at a time.
Bounded FIFO queue of capacity K. enqueue blocks when full; dequeue blocks when empty. Safety: 0 <= len <= K; FIFO order preserved.
Bounded LIFO stack with push/pop. Safety: size <= K; pop returns the last pushed element.
Single-producer single-consumer ring buffer with head/tail indices modulo K. Safety: tail catches head only when empty; head catches tail only when full.
Priority queue over a small priority set {high, med, low} with bounded counts per level. Pop returns highest non-empty. Safety: pop never returns a lower priority while a higher exists.
Bounded deque with push/pop at both ends. Safety: 0 <= size <= K; push_front then pop_front returns the same value.
Bounded set with insert / remove / contains modeled as a finite subset of a small universe. Safety: contains(x) iff inserted(x) and not removed since.
Bounded LRU cache of capacity K modeled as a sequence of distinct keys. get bumps to MRU; put may evict LRU. Safety: cache len <= K; all keys distinct.
Bounded FIFO cache of capacity K. Insert evicts oldest when full. Safety: insertion order preserved among current entries.
Abstract Bloom filter with a small bit set and 2 hash functions over a small universe. Insert sets bits; query is positive iff all bits set. Safety: no false negatives.
Disjoint-set union-find over a small universe. union merges component reps; find returns rep. Safety: components form a partition; union is monotone (components only merge).
Bounded binary min-heap over a small element set. insert and extract-min preserve heap order modeled as: extract-min returns the current minimum. Safety: returned value <= all remaining.
Multi-producer multi-consumer circular buffer with head/tail counters and a wrap. Safety: head <= tail; tail - head <= K.
Singly linked list bounded to N nodes with insert-front and delete-by-value. Safety: list length <= N; deleted value no longer in list.
Bounded trie over a tiny alphabet and word-length cap. insert and lookup over a small word set. Safety: lookup(w) iff inserted(w).
Consistent hashing ring over N nodes and a small key universe. Each key routes to its successor on the ring. Safety: every key mapped to exactly one alive node.
Bounded key-value store over a small key universe. put / get / delete. Safety: get(k) returns the most recent put or NONE after delete.
Bounded versioned KV store: put bumps version; get returns the highest version. Safety: returned version is the maximum put version for that key.
Bounded multiset with bounded multiplicities. add increments, remove decrements (clamped at 0). Safety: per-element counts in 0..K.
Abstract skip-list: a sorted set with insert / delete / contains over a small universe. Safety: order preserved; contains iff inserted and not removed.
Counting Bloom filter where bits are small counters. insert increments, delete decrements. Safety: counts >= 0; counts <= maxInserts.
Two-phase locking on a single resource: transactions acquire S or X locks. Safety: at most one X; X excludes S.
OCC: read phase with read-set and write-set, validate at commit, abort on conflict. Track committed timestamps and abort flag. Safety: committed transactions are serializable.
Snapshot isolation MVCC over a single key with versioned writes. Readers see snapshot at start. Safety: a snapshot read returns a consistent version; writers conflict-detect.
2PL on two resources: classic deadlock via opposite acquisition order. Track per-txn waits-for. Safety: detect cycle implies deadlock; with ordered acquisition no deadlock.
Write-ahead log: records appended before applied to data; replay reapplies committed records. Safety: recovered data state is consistent with committed log prefix.
Idempotent write protocol with request ids: server dedupes by id. Safety: same id processed at most once even with retries.
Atomic bank transfer between two accounts: debit + credit must commit together. Safety: total balance conserved; no negative balances.
Lease-based distributed lock with expiry. Holder must renew before timeout. Safety: at most one holder at any logical time.

Saga pattern: forward steps with compensating actions. On failure of step k, run compensations for steps k-1..1 in reverse. Safety: either all forward steps committed or all executed forwards have been compensated.
Transactional outbox: writes to DB and outbox in one transaction; relay publishes outbox messages to a bus. Safety: published set is the set of committed outbox rows.
Client retries with an idempotency key; server stores key->result. Safety: same key returns same result; effects applied at most once.
Compare-and-swap on a version field: update succeeds only if version matches. Safety: lost updates impossible; version monotone.
Serializable snapshot isolation: detect rw-antidependency cycles and abort one. Safety: no rw-cycle in committed history.
Replicated append-only log over N replicas with majority commit. Safety: committed prefix is on a majority forever.
Leader lease for safe reads: a leader may serve local reads only inside its lease. Safety: at most one leader holds an unexpired lease.
Fencing token: lock service issues monotone tokens; storage rejects writes with stale tokens. Safety: an accepted write has token >= all previously accepted.
Bounded request queue with FIFO commit. Safety: commit order matches enqueue order.
Two-replica system with a witness. Either replica may serve only when paired with the witness. Safety: never two writers concurrently.
Chain replication with head, middle, tail. Writes flow head->tail; reads at tail. Safety: tail's state is a prefix of head's.
Quorum read with read-repair: read from r replicas, return latest, repair stale. Safety: returned value is the most recent value durable on the read quorum.
Round-robin scheduler over N tasks with a single CPU. Tasks rotate by index. Safety: at most one running task; rotation order preserved.
Preemptive priority scheduler with priorities {1,2,3}. Higher preempts lower. Safety: running task has the highest priority among ready.
MLFQ with 3 levels: a task drops a level after exhausting its quantum. Safety: tasks always in exactly one level; level decreases monotonically until I/O.
Per-task CPU quota: a task may run at most Q units per period. Safety: per-task usage <= Q in any period.

Dijkstra's dining philosophers with N philosophers and N forks; left-then-right with one philosopher reversed to avoid deadlock. Safety: no two adjacent philosophers eating; deadlock-free under the asymmetry.
Cigarette smokers problem with one agent and three smokers. Safety: a smoker smokes only when it has all three ingredients.
Sleeping barber with N waiting chairs. Customers leave if all chairs full. Safety: barber serves at most one customer at a time; waiters <= N.
Banker's algorithm with two resource types and N processes. A request is granted only if the resulting state has a safe sequence. Safety: only safe states are reachable.
Multiple processes acquiring multiple resources in a fixed total order. Safety: no deadlock cycle; mutual exclusion per resource.
Worker pool of W threads pulling tasks from a bounded queue. Safety: in-flight tasks <= W; pulled tasks not visible in queue.
Token-bucket rate limiter with capacity K and refill 1/tick. Safety: per-window admitted requests <= K + ticks.
Leaky-bucket shaper: queue length bounded by K, drains 1/tick. Safety: queue <= K; output rate <= 1/tick.
Admission control: admit a request only if active count < capacity. Safety: active <= capacity at all times.
Per-tenant quota over a small tenant set. Each tenant capped at C concurrent ops. Safety: per-tenant in-flight <= C.
Bounded channel with explicit credit-based backpressure. Sender may transmit only if credits > 0. Safety: in-flight <= initial credits.
EDF scheduler with N tasks each having a deadline. Safety: at any time the running task has the smallest remaining deadline.
Resource lease with bounded duration: holder loses access after lease ticks expire. Safety: at most one holder; holder ticks <= lease.
Batch admission: requests are admitted in batches of up to B per tick. Safety: per-tick admitted <= B.
Per-flow fair queueing across F flows with round-robin dequeue. Safety: dequeue rotates flows; no starvation in a window of F.
Wait-for-graph cycle detector over N processes and M locks. Detects cycles among current waits. Safety: no cycle implies no deadlock.
MSI cache coherence with two cores and one cache line. States Modified, Shared, Invalid. Safety: at most one Modified; Modified excludes Shared.
MESI cache coherence with two cores. Modified/Exclusive/Shared/Invalid. Safety: at most one M or E line; M and E mutually exclusive across cores.
Per-core write buffer of bounded size. Stores commit FIFO. Safety: buffer length <= K; visible memory equals retired-store sum.
TSO store buffer model: one core's stores are FIFO and not visible until drained. Safety: drained stores reach memory in program order.
Two-thread store buffer with explicit MFENCE. Safety: an MFENCE drains the buffer; reads after MFENCE see globally visible stores.
TLB shootdown across N cores: invalidate request must be acked by all before completion. Safety: completed shootdown implies every TLB has invalidated.
Clock page replacement over K frames with reference bits. Eviction picks first frame with ref=0, clearing on the way. Safety: at most K resident pages.
LRU page replacement: K frames; on miss evict least recently used. Safety: |resident| <= K.
Two-socket NUMA model: per-socket memory and a remote-access latency abstracted as a flag. Safety: cross-socket reads always see the latest socket-local writes once propagated.
DMA engine: software issues a descriptor; engine transfers and raises an interrupt. Safety: completed transfer's destination matches descriptor source.
Reference-counted GC over a small object graph. inc/dec ref; object freed when ref=0. Safety: no live object freed; freed objects unreachable.
Mark-and-sweep GC over a small object graph. Mark from roots, sweep unmarked. Safety: any reachable object survives sweep.
Tricolor incremental GC with white/gray/black sets. Safety: no black object points to white; reachable objects don't end white.
Stop-the-world copying GC with from-space and to-space. Safety: after copy every reachable object lives in to-space and from-space is empty.
Per-CPU object cache (slab-style) feeding from a global pool. Safety: total objects in caches + pool = constant.
Bump-pointer arena with reset. Allocations move a pointer; reset sets it to 0. Safety: pointer in 0..ArenaSize.
Free-list allocator over K blocks. malloc takes a free block, free returns one. Safety: a block is in exactly one of {used, free}.
Simplified buddy allocator with two size classes. Splits and coalesces buddies. Safety: never two free buddies of the same size in adjacent halves of a parent.
Directory-based coherence with one directory and two caches. Directory tracks sharers. Safety: at most one writer; writer is in sharers list.
Write-through cache with one cache and main memory. Every write hits both. Safety: cached value matches memory after every write.
Order workflow: created -> paid -> shipped -> delivered, with cancel allowed only before shipped. Safety: delivered implies previously shipped and paid.
Payment processing: pending -> authorized -> captured | voided -> refunded. Safety: refunded implies captured; voided implies not captured.
OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow: client, auth server, resource. Tokens issued only after consent. Safety: a resource access implies a valid access_token from a consented session.
JWT session with issue/refresh/expire. Safety: an accepted token has not expired and has not been revoked.
Email verification: unverified -> code_sent -> verified. Codes single-use and time-limited. Safety: verified implies a matching unexpired code was used.
Password reset workflow: token issued, used once, time-bounded. Safety: a successful reset consumed an unexpired token.
TOTP 2FA: factor1 ok -> factor2 ok -> session granted. Safety: session implies both factors verified within window.
Refund workflow with partial refunds bounded by original amount. Safety: total refunded <= original; refunded only after captured.
Support ticket: open -> in_progress -> resolved -> closed, with reopen allowed from closed. Safety: resolved implies previously in_progress.
Merge-request workflow: draft -> ready -> approved -> merged. Reviewers required >= K. Safety: merged implies approved and approver count >= K.
Content moderation: submitted -> auto_review -> human_review -> approved | rejected. Safety: published iff approved.
Job scheduler with states queued, running, succeeded, failed, retried. Bounded retries. Safety: total retries <= K; succeeded is terminal.
Document approval requiring sequential signatures from N approvers in order. Safety: published implies signatures from all N in order.
User onboarding pipeline with steps: signup, email_verify, profile, terms. Each must complete before next. Safety: terms implies all prior steps completed.
Shopping cart with add/remove/checkout. Bounded item count and quantity. Safety: cart total <= max; checkout produces a single order.
Seat reservation: K seats; book/cancel; double-booking forbidden. Safety: per-seat, at most one active reservation.
Generic three-state workflow engine: state in {pending, active, done}. Track transitions and guards. Safety: transitions only along allowed edges.
Traffic-light FSM: red -> green -> yellow -> red. Track phase and tick counter. Safety: yellow follows green; never two adjacent lights green.
Door FSM: closed -> opening -> open -> closing -> closed. Sensors trigger transitions. Safety: opening implies previous state was closed; never both opening and closing.
Microwave FSM: idle, cooking, paused, finished. Door must be closed to cook. Safety: cooking implies door_closed; finished implies cooked time > 0.
Farmer with wolf, goat, cabbage crossing a river. Farmer can take at most one item. Safety: invariants forbid wolf+goat or goat+cabbage alone on a bank.
3 missionaries and 3 cannibals crossing a river in a 2-person boat. Safety: cannibals never outnumber missionaries on any bank with missionaries present.
Three couples crossing a river: no woman may be with another man unless her husband is present. Safety: husband-presence invariant.
Three-peg Towers of Hanoi for N disks. Track stacks as sequences. Safety: never a larger disk on a smaller one.
Two jugs of capacity 5 and 3 to measure 4 liters. Actions fill, empty, pour. Safety: jug levels stay within capacity.
Bounded queens: place K non-attacking queens on a K x K board incrementally. Safety: any two placed queens share neither row, column, nor diagonal.
Tic-tac-toe move legality: alternating X/O, only on empty squares, game ends on win or full board. Safety: at most one winner; turn count consistent with marks.
Sliding 15-puzzle reduced to 3x3 (8-puzzle). Track tile positions and blank index. Safety: tiles form a permutation of 0..8; blank moves only to adjacent.
Mini-sudoku 4x4 with one cell filled per move. Safety: any partially-filled grid has no row, column, or 2x2 region duplicates.
Knight's tour on a small 4x4 board. Track visited cells and current position. Safety: knight only moves L-shape; no cell visited twice.
Single chess king move legality on an 8x8 board. Safety: king moves only to adjacent cells and never onto an attacked square.
Peg solitaire on a small board. Each move jumps a peg over an adjacent peg into an empty cell, removing the jumped peg. Safety: peg count strictly decreases per move.
Rush Hour grid with cars of length 2 and 3 on a small board. Cars slide along their axes. Safety: cars never overlap; lengths preserved.
Lights Out 3x3 puzzle. Pressing a cell toggles itself and orthogonal neighbors. Track grid bits. Safety: each toggle changes 4 or 5 cells.
Place queens column by column on an N x N board (small N). Safety: no two queens attack each other.
A walker in a small grid maze with walls. Actions move N/S/E/W if no wall. Safety: walker stays inside the maze and never crosses walls.
Single face of a Rubik's cube as a 3x3 array of colors with rotate90 / rotate180 / rotate270. Safety: color multiset per face is invariant.
Blocks-world planning with 3 blocks: stack, unstack, pickup, putdown. Safety: a block is held by at most one hand; at most one block on each block.
Family river crossing puzzle: parents, children, policeman, prisoner with role-based boat constraints. Safety: prisoner never alone with family without policeman.
Three prisoners with hats of two colors guess their own. Bounded knowledge state per prisoner. Safety: a guess is announced only when justified by visible hats.
